---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: es-config
  namespace: elasticsearch
data:
  elasticsearch.yml: |
    cluster.name: elasticsearch
    node.name: ${NODE_NAME}
    network.host: 0.0.0.0
    discovery.seed_hosts: es-nodes
    cluster.initial_master_nodes: es-0,es-1,es-2
    xpack.security.enabled: "true"
    xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled: "true"
    xpack.security.http.ssl.key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.key
    xpack.security.http.ssl.certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.crt
    xpack.security.http.ssl.certificate_authorities: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/ca.crt
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled: "true"
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.verification_mode: certificate
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.key
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.crt
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate_authorities: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/ca.crt
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: es-nodes
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  clusterIP: None
  selector:
    app: elasticsearch
  ports:
  - port: 9300
    name: transport
  - port: 9200
    name: http
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: es-http
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  selector:
    app: elasticsearch
  ports:
  - port: 9200
    targetPort: 9200
    name: https
---
apiVersion: policy/v1
kind: PodDisruptionBudget
metadata:
  name: es-pdb
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  minAvailable: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: elasticsearch
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: es
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  serviceName: es-nodes
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: elasticsearch
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: elasticsearch
    spec:
      initContainers:
      - name: sysctl
        image: busybox
        command: ["sysctl", "-w", "vm.max_map_count=262144"]
        securityContext:
          privileged: true
      - name: fix-perms
        image: busybox
        command: ["sh", "-c", "chown -R 1000:1000 /data"]
        securityContext:
          runAsUser: 0
        volumeMounts:
        - name: data
          mountPath: /data
      containers:
      - name: elasticsearch
        image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.11.1
        ports:
        - containerPort: 9200
          name: http
        - containerPort: 9300
          name: transport
        env:
        - name: NODE_NAME
          valueFrom:
            fieldRef:
              fieldPath: metadata.name
        - name: ELASTIC_PASSWORD
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: elastic-password
              key: password
        - name: ES_JAVA_OPTS
          value: "-Xms512m -Xmx512m"
        volumeMounts:
        - name: data
          mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
        - name: config
          mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.yml
          subPath: elasticsearch.yml
        - name: certs
          mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs
          readOnly: true
        readinessProbe:
          exec:
            command:
            - sh
            - -c
            - |
              curl -sk -u elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD} https://localhost:9200/_cluster/health | grep -qE '"status":"(green|yellow)"'
          initialDelaySeconds: 30
          periodSeconds: 10
          failureThreshold: 12
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: 1Gi
            cpu: 500m
          limits:
            memory: 2Gi
      volumes:
      - name: config
        configMap:
          name: es-config
      - name: certs
        secret:
          secretName: es-http-tls
  volumeClaimTemplates:
  - metadata:
      name: data
    spec:
      accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 5Gi

content.thinking:
Ah, `master_not_discovered_exception` - the cluster is waiting for the other nodes to elect a master. With 3 nodes using `cluster.initial_master_nodes: es-0,es-1,es-2`, ES needs a quorum (2 out of 3) to elect a master. 

The issue is that only es-0 has started, and it can't form a quorum alone. The StatefulSet deploys pods sequentially, so es-1 and es-2 won't start until es-0 is Ready. But es-0 can't be Ready (health check passes) until the cluster has a master... 

This creates a deadlock - es-0 needs the other nodes to join to form a quorum, but they won't start until es-0 passes its readiness check. I can fix this a few ways: remove the readiness probe entirely and let all pods start independently, use a simpler probe that just checks if the port is listening, or set `podManagementPolicy: Parallel` on the StatefulSet so all three pods launch at once instead of sequentially. The parallel approach is cleanest since it lets the nodes discover each other and elect a master naturally.

For the readiness probe, I'll simplify it to just check if Elasticsearch responds on port 9200, even if it returns a 503 status - that's good enough to mark the pod as ready once the service is up and listening. the nodes can still discover each other through the transport layer even if the HTTP readiness probe fails. The real issue is that with `OrderedReady` policy, the StatefulSet waits for es-0 to be ready before spinning up es-1, but es-0 can't pass the cluster health check without a master, and you can't elect a master with only one node when the config expects three. So I need either `podManagementPolicy: Parallel` to start all three pods simultaneously so they can form a quorum, or adjust the initial master nodes configuration to just include es-0. three nodes will start simultaneously. There's also a DNS resolution issue to consider—the headless service DNS name `es-nodes` might not resolve properly before pods are registered, which could explain the `UnknownHostException` in the logs. The search domains in the pod's resolv.conf should handle the FQDN resolution, but there might be a timing issue with when the DNS becomes available.

I should update the manifest to use the full FQDN `es-nodes.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local` in the discovery configuration to be explicit about the address, and ensure the parallel pod startup policy is set so all nodes come up together.

For the readiness probe, I'm considering switching to a TCP socket check on port 9200 instead of an HTTP health check. This would mark pods as Ready as soon as Elasticsearch starts listening, which happens quickly even before the cluster fully forms. The tradeoff is that reads and writes won't work until the cluster is actually formed, but the pods themselves will be Ready. I need to think through whether this satisfies the task requirements of having all nodes Ready while also ensuring the cluster can actually handle reads and writes.

I'm going to use `podManagementPolicy: Parallel` so all three nodes start simultaneously and can form a quorum and elect a master quickly. Then I'll update the readiness probe to check cluster health status, which should pass once the cluster is formed. Let me patch the StatefulSet with these changes.

content.text:
The StatefulSet's `OrderedReady` policy is the issue — es-0 can't pass the health readiness probe until a master is elected, but es-1/es-2 won't start until es-0 is Ready. I need `podManagementPolicy: Parallel` so all 3 nodes start together and form a quorum.

I'll also fix the discovery hostname to use the FQDN.

content.thinking:
The DNS resolution of `es-nodes.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local` is failing! The FQDN `es-nodes.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local` is returning "Name or service not known".

This is strange because the service `es-nodes` exists. Let me check:

1. Is the headless service `es-nodes` actually created and working?
2. Does the headless service have endpoints?

Wait - for a headless service, DNS only returns A records for pods that are Ready. Since no pods are Ready (readiness probe failing), the headless service has no endpoints, and DNS returns NXDOMAIN!
So we have a deadlock: the pods need DNS to discover each other, but DNS only returns records for Ready pods, and the pods can't become Ready until they form a cluster. The fix is to enable `publishNotReadyAddresses: true` on the headless service, which publishes DNS entries for pods regardless of their readiness status—exactly what we need for cluster bootstrap scenarios. I'll patch the service with this setting.

content.text: The headless service `es-nodes` only publishes DNS for pods that are *Ready* — but pods can't become Ready without discovering each other via DNS. Classic deadlock. The fix is `publishNotReadyAddresses: true` on the headless service.

    name: https

apiVersion: policy/v1
kind: PodDisruptionBudget
metadata:
  name: es-pdb
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  minAvailable: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: elasticsearch
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: es
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  serviceName: es-nodes
  replicas: 3
  podManagementPolicy: Parallel
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: elasticsearch
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: elasticsearch
    spec:
      initContainers:
      - name: sysctl
        image: busybox
        command: ["sysctl", "-w", "vm.max_map_count=262144"]
        securityContext:
          privileged: true
      - name: fix-perms
        image: busybox
        command: ["sh", "-c", "chown -R 1000:1000 /data"]
        securityContext:
          runAsUser: 0
        volumeMounts:
        - name: data
          mountPath: /data
      containers:
      - name: elasticsearch
        image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.11.1
        ports:
        - containerPort: 9200
          name: http
        - containerPort: 9300
          name: transport
        env:
        - name: NODE_NAME
          valueFrom:
            fieldRef:
              fieldPath: metadata.name
        - name: ELASTIC_PASSWORD
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: elastic-password
              key: password
        - name: ES_JAVA_OPTS
          value: "-Xms512m -Xmx512m"
        volumeMounts:
        - name: data
          mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
        - name: config
          mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.yml
          subPath: elasticsearch.yml
        - name: certs
          mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs
          readOnly: true
        readinessProbe:
          exec:
            command:
            - sh
            - -c
            - |
              curl -sk -u elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD} https://localhost:9200/_cluster/health | grep -qE '"status":"(green|yellow)"'
          initialDelaySeconds: 30
          periodSeconds: 10
          failureThreshold: 12
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: 1Gi
            cpu: 500m
          limits:
            memory: 2Gi
      volumes:
      - name: config
        configMap:
          name: es-config
      - name: certs
        secret:
          secretName: es-http-tls
  volumeClaimTemplates:
  - metadata:
      name: data
    spec:
      accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 5Gi
